{"problem_standardized_math":"Consider the optimization problem\n\\[\n\\begin{array}{ll}\n\\text{minimize} & x^2 \\\\\n\\text{subject to} & c(x)=0,\n\\end{array}\n\\]\nwhere \\(x\\in\\mathbf{R}\\) and\n\\[\nc(x)=\n\\begin{cases}\nx^6\\sin(1/x) & \\text{if } x\\neq 0,\\\\\n0 & \\text{if } x=0.\n\\end{cases}\n\\]\nVerify that every feasible point other than \\(x=0\\) is an isolated local solution by showing that, for each such feasible point, there exists a neighborhood \\(\\mathcal{N}\\) in which it is the only feasible point."}
